verses.2: A day of darkness and gloominess,
verses.4: Their appearance is as the appearance of horses,
verses.5: Like the noise of chariots on the tops of the mountains, they leap,
verses.6: At their presence the peoples are in anguish.
verses.11: The LORD thunders his voice before his army,
verses.12: “Yet even now,” says the LORD, “turn to me with all your heart,
verses.13: Tear your heart and not your garments,
verses.14: Who knows? He may turn and relent,
verses.17: Let the priests, the ministers of the LORD, weep between the porch and the altar,
verses.18: Then the LORD was jealous for his land,
verses.20: But I will remove the northern army far away from you,
verses.22: Don’t be afraid, you animals of the field;
verses.23: “Be glad then, you children of Zion,
verses.24: The threshing floors will be full of wheat,
verses.25: I will restore to you the years that the swarming locust has eaten,
verses.26: You will have plenty to eat and be satisfied,
verses.27: You will know that I am among Israel,
verses.28: “It will happen afterward, that I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh;
verses.29: And also on the servants and on the handmaids in those days,
verses.30: I will show wonders in the heavens and in the earth:
verses.31: The sun will be turned into darkness,
verses.32: It will happen that whoever will call on the LORD’s name shall be saved;
